name: Excell Pepple
title: Technical Game Designer
sessionDescription: Learn the ins and outs of what it takes to become a game developer in today's rapidly evolving industry. This session explores indie development, self-publishing, building your portfolio, networking, and how aspiring developers can take meaningful next steps toward a career in games.
intendedAudience: Aspiring developers, students, hobbyists, and anyone interested in indie game development.

name: Nick Curto
title: Accessibility Engineer at Filament Games
sessionTitle: Making Accessible Games for Screen Readers and WCAG
sessionDescription: Learn how modern games can become more accessible to players using screen readers and assistive technologies. Nick Curto shares practical industry experience from working on educational games at Filament Games and explains how developers can begin implementing accessibility standards into their own projects.
intendedAudience: Aspiring developers, students, hobbyists, and anyone interested in indie game development.

name: Scott Hill
title: Writer & Solo RPG Designer
sessionTitle: The Imitation Game: The Art and Mechanics of World Building in Solo RPGs
sessionDescription: Explore how solo RPGs combine lightweight mechanics with deep storytelling possibilities. This session examines how creative nonfiction, personal experiences, and imagination can inspire compelling game worlds and player-driven narratives.
intendedAudience: Aspiring developers, students, hobbyists, and anyone interested in indie game development.
